text: A structured return-to-running program after ACL reconstruction (10 running sessions over 14 days with progression algorithm) is feasible, with 33 of 34 participants completing the program.
Approximately half of participants experienced temporary exacerbation of symptoms during a structured return-to-running program after ACLR, but only one had to discontinue.
The IKDC subjective knee score is a significant predictor of successful return to running after ACLR, with an AUC of 80.4%.
An IKDC score above 64/100 should be used as a criterion before reintroducing running after ACLR, as it provides a 3-fold greater chance of short-term success.
The IKDC cut-off of 63.7/100 differentiated successful and unsuccessful return-to-running responders with 77.8% sensitivity and 75.0% specificity after ACLR.
Quadriceps and hamstring strength, step-down endurance, and modified Star Excursion Balance Test were not significant predictors of short-term return-to-running success after ACLR.
